title: mammea nervosa
intro: Mammea nervosa, also known as the yellow copahu or yellow copaiba, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest in South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of limonene, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Mammea nervosa has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antiviral and antifungal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's bark and resin are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that are used to treat various health conditions.
